Below you are provided with the current world model from the previous turn. The default behavior is to copy it and add or remove things based on the evidence that you gathered. BEFORE EXECUTING NEW ACTIONS YOU MUST ALWAYS GIVE THE REVISED VERSION OF THE WORLD MODEL.

When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: The board didn't change after the UP actions (board_changed: false), and it stopped early with noop_short_circuit. This means the blocks can't move UP anymore - they're at the top of their allowed range. Let me check the current position and think about what to do next.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check current block position - they can't go UP anymore
ascii_lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print("Top area:")
for i in range(8, 22):
    row_slice = ascii_lines[i][28:42]
    print(f"Row {i}: [{row_slice}]")

# Check yellow bar
for node in current_frame.segmentation['nodes']:
    if node['color'] == 'Y':
        print(f"\nYellow bar: pixels={node['pixels']}, boundary={node['boundary']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
